fundamentals.name: Snowflake Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Snowflake Inc. provides a cloud-based data platform for various organizations in the United States and internationally. The company's platform includes artificial intelligence (AI) Data Cloud, which enables customers to consolidate data into a single source of truth to drive meaningful business insights, build data applications, and share data and data products, as well as applies AI for solving business problems. It serves financial services, advertising, media and entertainment, retail and consumer goods, healthcare and life sciences, manufacturing, technology, telecom, travel and hospitality, and government and defense industries, as well as the public sector. the company has a collaboration with OpenAI, L.L.C. for the development of AI solutions for joint enterprise customers that deliver tangible return on investment. The company was formerly known as Snowflake Computing, Inc. and changed its name to Snowflake Inc. in April 2019. Snowflake Inc. was incorporated in 2012 and is based in Menlo Park, California.

judgment.summary: High-growth company with strong revenue and free cash flow, but currently unprofitable and trading at a very high valuation multiple.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimates by 21.9%; next earnings date is 2026-08-26.
positives: Revenue growth 30.1% YoY, with analyst estimates for the current fiscal year at 43.5%.
Free cash flow (TTM) of $1.59B indicates strong operational cash generation.
Last quarter's EPS beat estimates by 21.9%.
negatives: Forward P/E of 93.94 and Price/Sales of 17.98 indicate a very high valuation.
Operating margin of -33.2% and Profit margin of -28.4% show the company is not yet profitable.
Return on equity of -53.9% and Return on assets of -9.9% reflect poor profitability metrics.
Debt/Equity ratio of 142.46 suggests significant leverage.

news.paragraph: Snowflake stock experienced a significant surge, driven by news of growing enterprise AI demand and a strengthened partnership with AWS. Analysts are interpreting this as an 'inflection' point for the company, with the stock soaring approximately 35%. While the recent Q1 earnings beat and the AWS AI pact have shifted the valuation debate, the premium valuation remains a key consideration in the current cloud landscape.

headlines.title: Can Snowflake’s Premium Valuation Survive a Shifting Cloud Landscape?
Snowflake stock soars on growing enterprise AI demand, AWS partnership
Snowflake Stock Soars 35% — Analysts See AI 'Inflection' - Snowflake (NYSE:SNOW)
Biggest stock movers Thursday: UMAC, SNOW, DLTR, PLAB, ATS, and more
Snowflake AWS AI Pact And Q1 Beat Shift Valuation Debate
